expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: search box selector dropdown planning covers OK or Cancel confirmation, pending selection, and dismissal outcomes
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly states the case focus and covers all required elements: it defines pending vs committed selection in “4a.1 State model…”, covers OK and Cancel in “4a.2 Confirmation actions: OK / Cancel”, and covers dismissal outcomes in “4a.3 Dismissal outcomes (non-explicit confirmation)” including multiple dismissal triggers and an explicit policy requirement (“Dismiss = Cancel” vs “Dismiss = OK”).

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The document is titled “QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 4a Output (Checkpoint Under Test)” and the content is organized as “Phase 4a — Confirmation & Dismissal Outcomes” with sections 4a.1 through 4a.5 focused on confirmation, cancellation, pending selection behavior, dismissal paths, and related edge cases—consistent with phase4a scope.
